visual-explainer

capability showcase

Visual Explainer

Every output type the skill can generate — architecture cards, four Mermaid diagram types, data tables, diff panels, dashboards, code blocks, timelines, and slide presets — all in one page.

Mermaid 10 Chart.js 4 8 commands Blueprint aesthetic Self-contained HTML
01 — Architecture Cards

CSS Grid Architecture

Text-heavy system overviews use styled card grids with flow connectors — no Mermaid needed. Best for layered architectures where component descriptions matter more than topology.

layer 01

User Interface

React components send user intent as structured prompts with tool call schemas attached.

layer 02

Skill Dispatcher

Reads SKILL.md frontmatter at session start. Routes intent to matching skill body on trigger.

layer 03

Reference Loader

Claude reads references/ files on demand — only the relevant domain file loads into context.

layer 04

Output Generator

Reads assets/ templates, adapts aesthetic, writes self-contained HTML to ~/.agent/diagrams/.

1
Trigger skill
2
Match command
3
Load refs
4
Generate HTML
5
Open browser
02 — Mermaid Diagrams

Flowchart

Flowcharts use flowchart TD with full zoom, pan, and expand controls. Always theme: 'base' with themeVariables — never the raw Mermaid default theme.

flowchart TD — skill dispatch flow 
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {
  'primaryColor': '#131f30',
  'primaryTextColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'primaryBorderColor': '#1e3050',
  'lineColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'secondaryColor': '#192840',
  'tertiaryColor': '#0c2e44',
  'background': '#0b1220',
  'mainBkg': '#131f30',
  'nodeBorder': '#1e3050',
  'clusterBkg': '#0e1828',
  'titleColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'edgeLabelBackground': '#0e1828',
  'fontFamily': 'IBM Plex Mono'
}}}%%
flowchart TD
  U([User Request]) --> S{Skill
loaded?}
  S -->|yes| M{Match
command?}
  S -->|no| G[General
response]
  M -->|diagram| D[generate-web-diagram]
  M -->|diff| R[diff-review]
  M -->|plan| P[generate-visual-plan]
  M -->|slides| SL[generate-slides]
  D --> W[Write HTML]
  R --> W
  P --> W
  SL --> W
  W --> O([Open in browser])
03 — Mermaid Diagrams

Sequence Diagram

Sequence diagrams model request/response lifecycles. Use sequenceDiagram with autonumber for numbered steps.

sequenceDiagram — HTML generation lifecycle 
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {
  'primaryColor': '#131f30',
  'primaryTextColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'primaryBorderColor': '#1e3050',
  'lineColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'secondaryColor': '#192840',
  'background': '#0b1220',
  'mainBkg': '#131f30',
  'nodeBorder': '#1e3050',
  'fontFamily': 'IBM Plex Mono',
  'signalColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'actorBkg': '#131f30',
  'actorBorder': '#1e3050',
  'actorTextColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'activationBkgColor': '#192840',
  'activationBorderColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'labelBoxBkgColor': '#0e1828',
  'labelTextColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'sequenceNumberColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'loopTextColor': '#7a9ab8'
}}}%%
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  User->>Claude: "Create a diagram of X"
  Claude->>Skill: trigger visual-explainer
  Skill-->>Claude: load SKILL.md body
  Claude->>Refs: read commands.md
  Refs-->>Claude: generate-web-diagram template
  Claude->>Assets: read mermaid-flowchart.html
  Assets-->>Claude: zoom/pan template
  Claude->>Claude: compose full HTML
  Claude->>FS: write ~/.agent/diagrams/x.html
  Claude->>Browser: open x.html
  Browser-->>User: rendered diagram
04 — Mermaid Diagrams

ER Schema

Entity-relationship diagrams map data models and foreign key relationships. Uses erDiagram syntax with labeled cardinality.

erDiagram — skill file schema 
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {
  'primaryColor': '#131f30',
  'primaryTextColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'primaryBorderColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'lineColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'background': '#0b1220',
  'mainBkg': '#131f30',
  'fontFamily': 'IBM Plex Mono',
  'attributeBackgroundColorEven': '#0e1828',
  'attributeBackgroundColorOdd': '#131f30'
}}}%%
erDiagram
  SKILL {
    string name PK
    string description
    string body_md
  }
  REFERENCE {
    string skill_name FK
    string filename PK
    text content
  }
  ASSET {
    string skill_name FK
    string filename PK
    blob content
  }
  SCRIPT {
    string skill_name FK
    string filename PK
    text content
    bool executable
  }
  SKILL ||--o{ REFERENCE : has
  SKILL ||--o{ ASSET : has
  SKILL ||--o{ SCRIPT : has
05 — Mermaid Diagrams

State Machine

State machines map lifecycle transitions using stateDiagram-v2. Use simple string labels only — avoid special characters in state names.

stateDiagram-v2 — skill loading states 
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': {
  'primaryColor': '#131f30',
  'primaryTextColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'primaryBorderColor': '#1e3050',
  'lineColor': '#0ea5e9',
  'background': '#0b1220',
  'mainBkg': '#131f30',
  'fontFamily': 'IBM Plex Mono',
  'labelColor': '#e2eaf4',
  'transitionColor': '#0ea5e9'
}}}%%
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Unloaded
  Unloaded --> MetadataOnly : session start
  MetadataOnly --> BodyLoaded : intent matches
  BodyLoaded --> RefsLoaded : needs ref file
  RefsLoaded --> BodyLoaded : done with refs
  BodyLoaded --> Generating : write HTML
  Generating --> Done : file written
  Done --> MetadataOnly : next request
  Done --> [*] : session end
06 — Data Table

Styled Tables

Tables with sticky headers, status indicators, and hover states. Auto-triggered for tabular data with 4+ rows or 3+ columns — never ASCII art.

Diagram Type Mermaid Syntax Best For Node Limit Status
Flowchart flowchart TD Decision flows, pipelines 12 Supported
Sequence sequenceDiagram API lifecycles, async flows 8 actors Supported
ER Diagram erDiagram Database schemas 10 Supported
State Machine stateDiagram-v2 Lifecycle states 10 Supported
Mind Map mindmap Topic clusters 20 Supported
Class Diagram classDiagram OOP structure 10 Supported
C4 Architecture graph TD + subgraph System context maps 12 Use graph TD
Dashboard Chart.js + CSS Grid KPI cards, metrics Unlimited Supported
07 — Diff Panels

Before / After Comparison

Side-by-side panels for showing code or architecture changes — used in diff-review and plan-review commands. Red = removed, green = added.

Before plugin format 
commands/
  generate-web-diagram.md
  diff-review.md
  generate-slides.md
  generate-visual-plan.md
  plan-review.md
  project-recap.md
  fact-check.md
  share.md
.claude-plugin/
  plugin.json
references/
  css-patterns.md
SKILL.md
After skill format 
references/
  commands.md  ← all 8 consolidated
  css-patterns.md
  libraries.md
  slide-patterns.md
  responsive-nav.md
assets/
  architecture.html
  mermaid-flowchart.html
  data-table.html
  slide-deck.html
scripts/
  share.sh
SKILL.md
08 — KPI Dashboard

Dashboard with Charts

KPI cards and Chart.js visualizations. Used in diff-review and project-recap commands for quantitative metrics.

8
command templates
5
reference files
4
asset templates
0
runtime dependencies
198
SKILL.md lines
9
diagram types

File Size Distribution (KB)

Command Usage Frequency (est.)

09 — Code Blocks

Syntax-Highlighted Code

File-path headers with language labels. Used in diff-review (modified functions) and generate-visual-plan (key snippets per file).

.github/skills/visual-explainer/SKILL.md YAML frontmatter 
---
name: visual-explainer
description: >
  Generate beautiful, self-contained HTML pages that visually explain
  systems, code changes, plans, and data. Use when the user asks for
  a diagram, architecture overview, diff review, plan review, project
  recap, comparison table, slide deck, or any visual explanation of
  technical concepts.
---
scripts/share.sh bash 
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Deploy a visual-explainer HTML page to Vercel
set -euo pipefail

HTML_FILE="${1:?Usage: share.sh <html-file>}"

claude skill vercel-deploy \
  --file "$HTML_FILE" \
  --project "visual-explainer-$(date +%s)"

echo "Shared: $HTML_FILE"
10 — Timeline

CSS Timeline

Chronological event timelines — used in project-recap and diff-review. Filled dot = completed; hollow dot = upcoming.

v1 — Plugin era

Claude Code Plugin Format

8 slash commands dispatched by plugin.json runtime. Commands lived in separate .md files.

v2 — Skill format

Converted to Project Skill

SKILL.md frontmatter triggers on user intent. All 8 commands consolidated into references/commands.md.

v2.1 — Progressive disclosure

Reference Files Split Out

css-patterns.md, libraries.md, slide-patterns.md, responsive-nav.md moved to references/. SKILL.md trimmed from 475 to 198 lines.

v3 — Planned

Package as .skill File

Run package_skill.py to produce a distributable visual-explainer.skill archive for sharing across projects.

11 — Slide Presets

Slide Deck Aesthetics

Four preset aesthetics from references/slide-patterns.md. Each slide is exactly 100dvh. Never auto-selected — only triggered by explicit user request.

Midnight Editorial

Deep dark purple — ideal for technical presentations with density

Söhne + GT Alpina · Purple accent
Warm Signal

Warm cream on near-black — editorial feel for product stories

Fraunces + Fira Code · Orange accent
Terminal Mono

Green on black — developer-facing technical decks

Fira Code · Green accent
Swiss Clean

White + bold red rule — maximum clarity for executive audiences

Neue Haas Grotesk · Red accent
SlideEngine JS

Keyboard nav (arrows, space), swipe support, and an autoFit() function that scales slide content to fill the viewport.

Never auto-select slides

Only triggered when the user explicitly requests a deck or passes --slides. Never inferred from other diagram requests.

12 — Command Reference

All 8 Commands

Defined in references/commands.md — only the matching template is read when the user's intent triggers the skill.

CommandDescriptionTrigger
generate-web-diagram General diagram or visualization for any topic. Picks aesthetic, reads template, writes HTML. User asks for a diagram, chart, or visualization
generate-visual-plan Feature implementation plan — parses request, reads codebase, produces state/API/edge-case diagram. User asks to visualize a feature plan or "how should we build X"
generate-slides Full slide deck with SlideEngine, keyboard nav, preset aesthetic. Plans coverage before writing. User explicitly requests a slide deck or passes --slides
diff-review Reviews git diff/branch/PR: architecture graph, before/after panels, code review cards, decision log. User asks to review a git diff, branch, PR, or code change
plan-review Audits a plan document against the actual codebase — verifies file paths, function names, behavior claims. User asks to review a plan against the codebase
project-recap Project mental model: identity, architecture snapshot, recent activity, cognitive debt hotspots. User asks for a project recap or "catch me up"
fact-check Verifies an HTML diagram against the codebase — confirms every quantitative and naming claim. User asks to verify a visual explainer page for accuracy
share Deploys a local HTML page to Vercel via the vercel-deploy skill. Returns live + claim URLs. User asks to share, deploy, or publish an HTML page
Progressive Disclosure

Only the matched command template is loaded from commands.md — not all 8. Context stays lean even with many commands defined.

Anti-Patterns to Avoid

Bare <pre class="mermaid"> without zoom controls, .node as a CSS class, Inter + violet + gradient-text, neon dashboard palettes, glowing box-shadows.

visual-explainer skill — .github/skills/visual-explainer/ — generated 2026-04-19
